verdict_reason: Solid emulator-backed IT covering happy path, 400s and the BAD_GATEWAY send-failure path, but the AC clause that rejected bodies are 'not sent to ASB' is unasserted anywhere (Major), plus minor isolation/assertion/convention nits.

todos.issue: AC says a missing/malformed body 'is rejected with a 4xx response and is not sent to ASB' — both 400 tests assert only the status/body, never that nothing reached Service Bus, and no other test (unit or IT) pins that clause
todos.fix: In post_returnsBadRequest_forMissingBody and post_returnsBadRequest_forMalformedJson add Mockito.verifyNoInteractions(senderClient) (or assert the queue is empty) after the status assertion

todos.issue: receiveMessage() builds the receiver in the default PEEK_LOCK mode and never settles the received message, so after the lock expires the message returns to the shared static queue and can be redelivered into later tests/classes as the suite grows
todos.fix: Add .receiveMode(ServiceBusReceiveMode.RECEIVE_AND_DELETE) to the receiver builder (or call receiver.complete(received) before returning)

todos.issue: Happy-path test asserts the received body merely contains 'NotificationSubmitted' — a substring check would still pass if the gateway wrapped or mangled the payload, so it doesn't pin the AC's 'sends that content' forwarding fidelity
todos.fix: Assert the exact forwarded body (isEqualTo the posted JSON) and the message content type (received.getRawAmqpMessage()/getContentType() == application/json) which QueueMessageSender sets

todos.issue: No // Given / When / Then comment blocks in any test — the integration-testing guide says the comment blocks must be present even when When/Then are combined in a fluent chain
todos.fix: Add // Given, // When, // Then comments to each test method

todos.issue: Test names use post_returnsX_whenY instead of the documented {subject}_{shouldDoWhat}_{whenContext} pattern (e.g. post_shouldReturnAccepted_whenValidJson) — same deviation across all four tests
todos.fix: Rename tests to the should-form, e.g. post_shouldReturnAccepted_andMessageLandsInQueue, post_shouldReturnBadGateway_whenSendFails
